archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

evidence.detail: Re-export gateway concentrating all rex imports
Concurrency limiting infrastructure — shared utility with likely high fan-in from process management code
Monitors memory usage as an active service — encapsulates ongoing observation logic
Intercepts process execution based on memory pressure — middleware pattern between caller and execution
Process pool lifecycle management — orchestrates worker processes as a stateful service
Tracks per-process memory stats — shared utility consumed by monitor and throttle
Stateful execution queue managing task ordering and dispatch — service orchestration logic
Formatting helpers for queue output — pure transformation utility
Priority-based task scheduling logic — business logic orchestration for queue ordering
Shared glob pattern helpers — infrastructure utility with high fan-in potential
Defines tool interfaces/contracts — type definitions and constants with no logic
Routes tool calls to implementations — service-layer dispatch orchestration
Executes shell commands — low-level utility wrapping child_process
File system operations for tool use — shared utility module
Git operations for tool use — shared utility wrapping git CLI
Rex PRD integration tool — API client/business logic orchestration against rex package
Shell invocation helpers — low-level infrastructure utility
Orchestrates test execution as a tool — business logic for running and interpreting tests
Validates task completion criteria — business logic service, not a schema validator
Implements direct API calls to LLM provider — API client service
Authentication helpers for LLM API access — shared utility
Implements LLM provider via CLI subprocess — service encapsulating provider strategy
Codex-specific CLI provider implementation — concrete service strategy
Factory function for creating LLM clients — infrastructure utility
Execution helpers for subprocess invocation — low-level utility
Formats help/usage text — pure formatting utility
Core LLM client interface and implementation — primary service module
LLM configuration schema and defaults — configuration module
Type definitions and interfaces for LLM client — pure types file
Project-level config reading for LLM settings — configuration module
Helper module providing project directory path resolution — shared infrastructure with no business logic.
Pure interface and type definitions (LLMProvider, ProviderInfo, StreamChunk, etc.) with no runtime logic.
ProviderRegistry class with factory registration, selection, and singleton defaultRegistry — business logic orchestration for provider lifecycle.
Session management for LLM provider instances — stateful service logic.
Suggestion generation logic — API client / data-fetching service layer.
Parsing utilities for token usage from API/CLI formats — shared helper with no state.
Handles acknowledgement of analysis proposals — business logic orchestration.
Shared helpers and constants used across multiple analyze modules.
Guards against over-consolidation during analysis — domain logic service.
Decomposes PRD items via LLM — business logic orchestration service.
Deduplication logic — stateless utility used across analyze pipeline.
Diff computation between PRD states — pure utility with no side effects.
Extracts structured proposals from LLM output — parsing/data-fetching service.
File validation helpers for the analyze pipeline — shared utility.
Orchestrates guided (interactive) analysis workflow — business logic service.
Bridge between rex analyze pipeline and LLM client — API client abstraction.
LLM-powered reasoning for modification proposals — domain logic service.
Generates PRD proposals from analysis — core business logic orchestration.
Core LLM reasoning module used throughout analyze — shared service layer.
Reconciles LLM proposals against existing PRD — domain logic service.
LLM reasoning for PRD reshape/reorganize proposals — domain service.
Scanner implementations that read project files for analysis — data-fetching service.
Validation helpers for modification proposals — stateless utility.
CLI help text strings — shared utility with no logic.
MCP tool handler implementations — business logic orchestration layer between MCP transport and rex domain core.
CLI command that sets up and starts the MCP server — command handler.
CLI input validation helpers — stateless utility used by command handlers.
Detects conflicts between recommendations and existing PRD — domain logic service.
Creates PRD items from sourcevision recommendations — business logic orchestration.
Exports a single DEFAULT_WORKFLOW string constant — pure constant/config value with no logic.
Exports BUILTIN_ARCHETYPES constant array with ArchetypeDefinition objects — pure data/type definitions, no logic.
Business logic service that classifies PRD items by significance and breaking-change status using heuristics.
Orchestrates collecting completed rex PRD items from disk and git history into enriched work items.
Pure filtering utility — timestamp-based predicate logic for filtering work items by branch lifecycle.
Persistent JSON storage layer for branch work records with Zod validation — stateful store pattern.
Generates structural findings (god functions, dead code, hub functions) from call graph data.
Extracts call graph via TypeScript AST — builds function definitions and cross-file call relationships.
File classification engine matching weighted signals against archetypes — core domain logic service.
LLM bridge service — vendor-aware API client wrapping @n-dx/llm-client with module-level config state.
Reads and extracts PRD completion status and metadata from prd.json — data access service.
AST-based analyzer for React component definitions, JSX usage graph, and route detection.
Generates CONTEXT.md output — structured text generation service for AI consumption.
Tracks zone metric snapshots across runs and computes deltas — stateful convergence tracking service.
Handles batch AI enrichment with retry logic and meta-evaluation — orchestration service.
Exports constants and PassConfig type definitions for enrichment pipeline — pure config/types.
Pure parsing utilities — tryParseJSON, extractFindings, markdown fence extraction from Claude responses.
Per-zone AI enrichment orchestration — parallelizable LLM calls with focused per-zone context.
Barrel re-export aggregator for the enrichment pipeline modules — re-export-heavy gateway pattern.
Deterministic import graph analyzer using TypeScript compiler API — core analysis phase service.
Filesystem walker that catalogs files into structured inventory — core analysis phase service.
Generates llms.txt structured Markdown output for LLM consumption — text generation service.
Louvain community detection algorithm for architectural zone identification — computational service.
CRUD operations for manifest JSON (readManifest, writeManifest) — data access and persistence service.
Generates file-move recommendations based on zone divergence and import neighbors — analysis service.
Derives and prioritizes actionable next steps from findings — business logic service.
Computes cohesion/coupling metrics and risk levels for zones — scoring/analysis service.
Detects HTTP route handlers from JSDoc, Express/Hono/Koa patterns — static analysis service.
Pure utility functions for token usage accumulation — stateless helpers with no domain logic.
Merges pre-analyzed repos into unified sourcevision output — aggregation orchestration service.
Resolves external npm imports to sibling workspace members as ZoneCrossing entries — analysis service.
Analyzer orchestration module — coordinates analysis pipeline logic
Computes deterministic hashes for zone content — pure utility function
Generates per-zone output files (context.md, summary.json) — I/O orchestration logic
Core analysis service implementing Louvain community detection for zone discovery — business logic orchestration, not a utility or model.
Help text rendering for CLI — no command dispatch, pure display utility
Implements the 'mcp' subcommand handler for the sourcevision CLI
Implements the 'serve' subcommand handler for the sourcevision CLI
Generates PDF report artifacts — export/transformation service
Template generator for PR markdown output — pure transformation, no state
Shared merge helper in util/ — classic utility module
Path manipulation helpers — shared infrastructure utility
Canonical deterministic sorting functions — pure utility, high fan-in
esbuild-based build configuration script that defines entry points, output directories, and build options.
Landing module entry point — top-level wiring for the landing bundle
In-process caching layer for aggregated data — stateful service
Tracks concurrent execution metrics — instrumentation service
Concentrates all cross-package imports from sourcevision — canonical gateway per CLAUDE.md
Computes incremental token usage per task — business logic service
Port resolution/management utility — shared infrastructure helper
Diagnostics collection for PR markdown refresh — observability service
PRD file I/O operations — data access service layer
Tracks process memory over time — instrumentation/monitoring service
Registers scheduled jobs/background tasks — lifecycle orchestration service
Concentrates all cross-package imports from rex — canonical gateway per CLAUDE.md
Express route handler for rex analysis endpoints
Express route handler for rex execution endpoints
Express route handler for health check endpoints
Express route handler for rex item CRUD endpoints
Express route handler for rex prune operation endpoint
Server-side HTTP route handler for rex read endpoints
Server-side HTTP route handler for rex requirements endpoints
Shared utilities and helpers for rex route handlers
Search index management — data fetching and business logic
Shared type definitions for server layer
Task usage tracking service — business logic orchestration
Periodic background service that orchestrates cleanup of orphaned usage records across aggregator, PRD, and WebSocket broadcast.
WebSocket connection handling middleware in the server layer
Data file path constants and definitions — no logic, framework-agnostic
Shared utility for culling graph nodes — infrastructure helper
One-time application startup module that initializes theme, polling infrastructure, and tab visibility — a setup entry point with side effects.
Standalone, framework-free module providing crash detection and navigation state persistence utilities consumed across the viewer.
Explicitly an intra-package gateway per CLAUDE.md — re-exports from messaging/, shared/, schema/ for viewer consumers
Graph physics calculations — pure utility with no framework dependencies
Graph rendering logic — infrastructure utility for the visualization layer
Data loading and fetching service for the viewer
Rate-limiting middleware in the viewer messaging pipeline
Fetch request pipeline middleware — intercepts and processes outbound requests
Message coalescing middleware — batches rapid messages before dispatch
Message throttling middleware — controls message flow rate
Request deduplication middleware — prevents redundant in-flight requests
WebSocket message pipeline middleware for the viewer
Pure re-export shim that forwards all exports from the relocated crash-detector — a backward-compatibility gateway with no logic.
DOM performance monitoring utility — observes and reports metrics
Intercepts and gates DOM update scheduling calls based on tab visibility — middleware in the render pipeline between coalescer and batcher.
Graceful degradation utility — manages feature reduction under load
Refresh throttling utility — limits redraw frequency
Sits at the front of the WebSocket message pipeline, gating/dropping messages during tab inactivity — classic pipeline middleware.
Update batching utility — coalesces state updates before applying
Standalone module with no framework deps that batches tick state updates via RAF — pure infrastructure utility sitting between tick-timer and useTick hook instances.
Orchestrates polling intervals across the application with tab-visibility integration — a service that manages lifecycle of registered pollers.
Coordinator bridge between graceful-degradation and polling-state; pure infrastructure glue with no framework dependencies.
Centralized registry of all polling sources with suspend/resume/dispose coordination — a state store with high fan-in from polling-manager, tick-timer, memory-monitor, etc.
Wraps the Page Visibility API into a single-source-of-truth module; stateless helper with event subscription — utility infrastructure consumed by multiple polling modules.
Shared timer service managing a single setInterval distributed to subscribers — service that auto-starts/stops based on subscriber count.
Infrastructure bridge between tab-visibility and tick-timer; pure coordinator with no framework dependencies and no exported state.
Applies schema version migrations to loaded data; pure transformation logic with no framework or state dependencies.
Shared viewer path-display helpers plus backward-compat re-exports from visualization/ — canonical shared utility module.
Zone color palette constants and lookup helpers; pure functions and data with high fan-in from visualization components.
Pure data-transformation functions that build node/edge arrays from zone/call data for FlowDiagram components.
Tiny module of pure classification helpers (meterClass) for metric display — shared visualization utility.
Top-level CI/PR gate script that spawns build and validate steps and exits with a status code — a runnable orchestration entry point.
Exports helper functions for writing dashboard artifact metadata files — shared utility consumed by the refresh pipeline.
Exports flag-parsing and plan-building logic for the refresh command — pure transformation utility with no side effects.
Exports snapshot/rollback helpers for sourcevision output files during refresh — shared infrastructure utility.
Orchestration entry point for server start/stop/status — explicitly listed in CLAUDE.md as one of the four orchestration entry points; spawns @n-dx/web serve.
